text: Large language models, or LLMs, are a type of artificial intelligence trained on massive amounts of text from the internet, books, and articles. They learn patterns in language by predicting the next word in a sentence. When you give an LLM a prompt, like a question or instruction, it generates a response based on everything it has learned. This is called prompt engineering—crafting inputs to get better outputs. LLMs can write essays, answer questions, and even create stories. However, they have limits: they can make mistakes, cannot truly understand meaning, and may repeat biased information from their training data. They are powerful tools, but not perfect.
